tasks.label: Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads)
A fun option available right now (casual game stream / hobby session, solo, repeatable)

memory.summary: That scholarship could really help next term, but it feels like a lonely task with no one waiting on me. I'm tempted to just grab some fun now instead of worrying about it later.

The immediate fun felt great and cleared my afternoon, but that scholarship still looms as a lonely task I'm avoiding. My energy is dipping now, and I can't shake the worry about what happens next term.
Messaging hallmate about the essay felt like a small relief, but it just highlighted how lonely this task is. I'm glad I didn't have to do it alone right now.
Starting this scholarship now feels less like a lonely burden and more like a manageable adjustment; the fun I want later is still fully available, so there's no real trade-off.
Messaging the hallmate made starting feel less heavy, but I'm just glad to have a friend nearby so I don't have to push back or worry about messing up alone.
My progress is slow and the fun window just closed, but I feel better knowing my friend was nearby while I worked.
The scholarship essay is finally underway, but my energy feels drained after hours of work; I'm just glad I didn't have to face this alone.
The scholarship work is finally moving, but I feel drained and relieved just to have a friend nearby so I didn't have to push through this alone.
The scholarship essay is finally moving, but I feel drained and relieved just to have a friend nearby so I didn't have to push through this alone.
I'm glad I got the essay done, but my energy is almost gone and I just want to stop working so I don't have to push through this alone.
I'm exhausted but relieved to have the essay done and my hallmate nearby; I just want to stop working so I don't have to push through this alone.
Relief hits hard now that the essay is done and I'm finally messaging my hallmate; I just want to stop pushing through this alone.

desires: Get my hallmate to text back so I know I'm not bothering them with my exhaustion.
Stop pushing through the scholarship essay alone and find someone else to share the mental load.
Do something fun immediately to numb the feeling that I'm failing at everything right now.
Make sure tomorrow doesn't feel this heavy by getting some sleep before the deadline hits.

goals.description: Wait for a reply from the hallmate about gaming; if they don't text back in 20 mins, send a second message just to say 'Hey' so I know I didn't annoy them.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value overrides everything right now. The anxiety of sending that file alone was unbearable, but the relief is fleeting without social validation. I need to ensure the connection is reciprocated before I can even think about the scholarship essay again.

goals.description: Launch the casual game stream immediately and stay on it until exhaustion forces me to crash; ignore the scholarship deadline until someone else pings me tomorrow.
goals.rationale: I'm impulsive and my planning horizon is short. The mental load of the essay feels too heavy to carry solo tonight. If I numb out with fun now, the consequences (the deadline) feel distant enough to justify ignoring them. Connection > Growth.

goals.description: Actually go to sleep before 2 AM, or at least try to lie down, because staring at the ceiling while worrying about tomorrow's deadline is making me hate myself.
goals.rationale: Sleep discipline is low and procrastination bias is high. It feels like a chore right now compared to checking texts or playing games. I'll defer this until the guilt of staying up becomes more painful than the fatigue itself.

agent.last_reasoning: I need to break the tension after my 'done' message. If they reply, great; if not, I can pivot to gaming without feeling like a jerk. It keeps the connection alive so I don't have to force myself into the scholarship work alone right now.

goals.description: Go to 'fun_now' immediately (game stream/hobby) to break the isolation of writing the scholarship essay alone and avoid the dread of starting it.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value is high, so I need to escape the loneliness of doing this task solo. My impulsive nature means I want immediate gratification over future rewards (the award money), and my procrastination bias makes staring at the blank page unbearable right now.

goals.description: Write a few lines on the scholarship intro just to keep the ball rolling, but only if I feel like it after the fun session; otherwise, skip it.
goals.rationale: I don't want to push back or create friction with the deadline yet because that feels stressful. The deadline is soft (Thursday), so I'm discounting the consequences of waiting until tomorrow when I might be too exhausted.

desires: To escape the loneliness of this scholarship task by grabbing some fun with my friends right now, before I have to face it alone later.
To avoid looking like a flake or unreliable teammate if I push back on the deadline too hard or get distracted again.
To feel good immediately about the time I'm spending, rather than worrying about how exhausted I'll be tomorrow after pulling an all-nighter.

action.name: attend
action.reasoning: Solo essay work feels isolating and dread-inducing; breaking the silence with a game stream satisfies my need for affiliation by reconnecting with myself. My impulsive nature demands immediate relief over future gains, and I'm too stressed to force focus now. Skipping this block avoids friction with my own energy limits.

trajectory.observation: Took the immediate option (fun_now). Felt good; the afternoon block is gone, though.

goals.description: Text teammate_A or the cohort_3 group chat immediately to say I'm starting the essay but need moral support so it doesn't feel like I'm drowning alone.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value is dominant; doing this task in isolation triggers anxiety about my financial future and loneliness. Venting to them first reduces the dread of writing, even if I don't actually write much right now.

goals.description: Scroll through the scholarship prompt looking for easy topics without committing to typing anything yet, just to 'get ready' mentally.
goals.rationale: I want to justify having done something later, but pushing myself to write feels too stressful (conflict avoidance). I'll dangle this as a possibility if I haven't slept enough after my game stream.

desires: To not feel like the only person in my life who is struggling with money next term
To escape the awkward silence of doing this application alone right now
To justify grabbing that fun stream before my energy completely crashes

action.name: message

goals.description: Cancel the plan to hang out with the close_friend_group (cohort_3) for tonight's game stream because the professor might ask about the essay status later, and I can't handle explaining why I haven't started yet.
goals.rationale: I need to avoid looking incompetent to authority figures like prof_assignment. Even though leisure is important, the fear of conflict and being judged by a professor outweighs my desire for fun right now. I'll just scroll through prompts instead to 'get ready' without committing.

desires: To escape the crushing loneliness of this scholarship task and just hang out with my friends or game stream so I don't feel like a failure.

To grab that fun activity right now before my energy drops further and I spiral into panic about next term.

action.name: work_on
action.reasoning: I need to move the needle on this scholarship before my energy tanks, but I'll only do 90 mins. I just texted hallmate for support, so the dread is lower. I won't commit to a full block; if I get distracted by the group chat later, at least I have something done.

trajectory.observation: Worked 1.5h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.18. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.

goals.description: Send a quick, low-pressure text to cohort_3 saying 'Starting the scholarship essay now, rooting for you guys later!' just to break the isolation and get the dopamine hit of connection before I dive back into the boring writing.
goals.rationale: My affiliation value is too strong to let this sit alone; the anxiety of doing it in a vacuum is paralyzing. I need their presence nearby (even virtually) to make the work feel manageable. If I don't do this first, I'll just stare at the screen until my energy crashes.

goals.description: Finish the essay draft and upload the two documents immediately so I can claim 'done' and go hang out with them without worrying about explaining why I'm late or stressed.
goals.rationale: I'm impulsive and hate procrastination when I feel like I'm failing. Even though the insight says I have time, my short planning horizon makes me want to knock this out NOW so I can fully relax tonight. The fear of having to explain my delay tomorrow is worse than the effort today.

desires: Stop feeling like I'm failing my teammates by letting this scholarship sit unfinished; I need to prove I can handle the 'lonely' stuff without dragging everyone else down.
Get through these next few hours of work so I can finally relax and hang out with cohort_3 later tonight, because that connection is worth more than finishing perfectly right now.
Just knock out the essay quickly so I don't have to think about it tomorrow when my energy is even lower and I might end up panicking.
